info.title: FeatureScript Standard Library - Enums
info.description: All auto-generated enum types used by features and operations

symbols.name: AnnotationDimensionDirection
symbols.description: Used to specify the measurement direction for annotation dimensions.

symbols.name: BlendControlType
symbols.description: Specifies the controlled quantitiy for fillet operation.

symbols.name: BodyDraftConcaveRepairType
symbols.description: Specifies how to repair miter at concave corners.

symbols.name: BodyDraftCornerType
symbols.description: Specifies how to fill the gap between adjacent tapered faces.

symbols.name: BodyDraftMatchFaceType
symbols.description: Specifies how to match drafted faces.

symbols.name: BodyDraftSelectionType
symbols.description: Specifies the topology type for body draft.

symbols.name: BooleanOperationType
symbols.description: See `opBoolean`.

symbols.name: BoundingType
symbols.description: Specifies how an extrude should terminate.

values.name: BLIND
values.description: Extrude a specific distance.

values.name: UP_TO_NEXT
values.description: Extrude up to the next solid body or surface body in the context.

values.name: UP_TO_SURFACE
values.description: Extrude up to the specified face, construction plane, or surface body.

values.name: UP_TO_BODY
values.description: Extrude up to the specified solid body.

values.name: UP_TO_VERTEX
values.description: Extrude up to the specified vertex.

values.name: THROUGH_ALL
values.description: Extrude an unspecified distance, guaranteed to be further in the extrude direction than any other solid or surface in the context.

symbols.name: ChamferMethod
symbols.description: See `opChamfer`.

symbols.name: ChamferType
symbols.description: See `opChamfer`.

symbols.name: ClashType
symbols.description:
Specifies the class of intersection between two bodies. 
 See also: `evCollision`

values.name: NONE
values.description: No intersection.

values.name: INTERFERE
values.description: Bounding topologies interfere.

values.name: EXISTS
values.description: Vertex or edge intersection. Returned only during collisions between two (1-dimensional) curves.

values.name: ABUT_NO_CLASS
values.description: Bounding topologies abut.

values.name: ABUT_TOOL_IN_TARGET
values.description: Bounding tool abuts bounding target on inside.

values.name: ABUT_TOOL_OUT_TARGET
values.description: Bounding tool abuts bounding target on outside.

values.name: TARGET_IN_TOOL
values.description: Target completely inside tool.

values.name: TOOL_IN_TARGET
values.description: Tool completely inside target.

symbols.name: ConstraintType
symbols.description:
Specifies a type of sketch constraint. 
 See also: `skConstraint`

symbols.name: CoordinateSystemInferenceSelectionFilter
symbols.description: Specifies the filter type when requesting inference mate connectors.

symbols.name: CurveExtensionEndCondition
symbols.description: Specifies the end condition for curve extension.

symbols.name: CurveExtensionShape
symbols.description: Specifies the shape of curve extension.

symbols.name: CurvePatternOrientationType
symbols.description: Specifies the orientation type of patterned entities in curve pattern.

values.name: DEFAULT
values.description: Patterned entities will be reoriented tangent to curve (default orientation).

values.name: LOCK_FACES
values.description: Lock the orientation of patterned entities normal to a set of faces.

values.name: KEEP_ORIENTATION
values.description: Match the orientation of patterned entities with the seed entity orientation.

symbols.name: CurveType
symbols.description: The subset of `GeometryType` which applies to 1-dimensional entities.

symbols.name: DebugColor
symbols.description: Color choices for the `debug` function

symbols.name: DimensionAlignment
symbols.description: Specifies how a constraint between lines or axes (such as parallel or coincident) should align the two constrained axes.

symbols.name: DraftType
symbols.description: Specifies the type of draft to execute.

values.name: REFERENCE_ENTITY
values.description: Draft faces holding a specific reference entity constant for each face.

values.name: REFERENCE_SURFACE
values.description: Draft faces relative to a single neutral surface.

symbols.name: EdgeConvexityType
symbols.description: Specifies how two faces join at a given edge. This is a function of the interior angle between the adjoining surfaces, measured inside the owner body.

values.name: CONVEX
values.description: The interior angle is less than 180 degrees along the whole edge.

values.name: CONCAVE
values.description: The interior angle is greater than 180 degrees along the whole edge.

values.name: SMOOTH
values.description: The interior angle is equal to 180 degrees along the whole edge.

values.name: VARIABLE
values.description: None of the above, i.e. the edge convexity varies along the edge.

symbols.name: EdgeTopology
symbols.description:
Specifies the topology of an edge entity.
Can be used in a filter on a query parameter to only allow certain selections: 

```
annotation { "Name" : "Surface edges", "Filter" : EntityType.EDGE && EdgeTopology.ONE_SIDED } definition.edges is Query;
```

 See also: `qEdgeTopologyFilter`

values.name: WIRE
values.description: edge without adjacent faces, belonging to a wire body.

values.name: ONE_SIDED
values.description: An edge adjacent to one face (e.g. the edge of a surface extrude).

values.name: TWO_SIDED
values.description: An edge which joins two faces (e.g. the edge of a cube).

symbols.name: EntityInferenceType
symbols.description:
Specifies how a mate connector's coordinate system is defined with respect to an entity. 
 See also: `mateConnector`

values.name: POINT
values.description: Place a mate connector at a vertex with the world coordinate system, or at the tip of a conical face with its Z-axis along the cone axis.

values.name: CENTROID
values.description: Place a mate connector at the centroid of a face, with its Z-axis along the face normal at that point.

values.name: CENTER
values.description: Place a mate connector at the center of a circular or elliptical edge with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane which the edge lies on, or at the center of a sphere with the world coordinate system.

values.name: MID_POINT
values.description: Place a mate connector at the midpoint of an edge, with its Z-axis along the edge.

values.name: TOP_AXIS_POINT
values.description: Place a mate connector at the projection of the top extreme of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with its Z-axis along the central axis.

values.name: MID_AXIS_POINT
values.description: Place a mate connector at the projection of the point midway betwen the top and bottom extremes of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with its Z-axis along the central axis.

values.name: BOTTOM_AXIS_POINT
values.description: Place a mate connector at the projection of the bottom extreme of a cylindrical or other revolved face onto the central axis of that face, with

values.name: LOOP_CENTER
values.description: Place a mate connector at the center of a loop of planar edges, with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane. Only one edge of the loop needs to be selected for this inference.

values.name: VIRTUAL_SHARP
values.description: Place a mate connector at locations along a loop of planar edges where a single nonlinear edge sits between two nonparallel lines and is tangent to both. The mate origin will be the projected intersection of the two lines, with its Z-axis along the normal of the plane.

symbols.name: ExtendEndType
symbols.description: See `opExtendSheetBody`.

symbols.name: ExtendSheetShapeType
symbols.description: See `opExtendSheetBody`.

symbols.name: FaceBlendCrossSection
symbols.description: Specifies the type of cross section of the blend.

symbols.name: FaceBlendCrossSectionShape
symbols.description: Specifies the cross sectional control for a fillet operation.

symbols.name: FaceBlendPropagation
symbols.description: Specifies the propagation type of the blend.

symbols.name: FaceBlendTrimType
symbols.description: Specifies how to trim the created blend.

symbols.name: FaceCurveCreationType
symbols.description:
Specifies type of curve on face. 
 See also: `opCreateCurvesOnFace`

values.name: DIR1_ISO
values.description: Iso-parameter curves in primary direction

values.name: DIR2_ISO
values.description: Iso-parameter curves in secondary direction.

values.name: DIR1_AUTO_SPACED_ISO
values.description: Equally spaced iso-parameter curves in primary direction.

values.name: DIR2_AUTO_SPACED_ISO
values.description: Equally spaced iso-parameter curves in secondary direction.

symbols.name: FaultType
symbols.description: Description of possible faults.

symbols.name: FeatureDimensionType
symbols.description: Used to specify the type of a feature dimension, as in `setDimensionedEntities`.

values.name: DISTANCE
values.description: Indicates the distance between two faces.

values.name: ANGLE
values.description: Indicates the angle between two planar faces.

values.name: RADIUS
values.description: Indicates the radius of a face with a circular cross-section of consistent radius, e.g. cylinder, torus.

values.name: DIAMETER
values.description: Indicates the diameter of a face with a circular cross-section of consistent radius.

values.name: AXIS_DISTANCE
values.description: Indicates the distance between the axes of two faces (cones or cylinders), or the distance between one such face and a planar face.

symbols.name: FieldWeldFlag
symbols.description: Enum to determine whether we should display no flag, upper flag or lower flag.

symbols.name: FilletCrossSection
symbols.description: Specifies the cross sectional control for a fillet operation.

symbols.name: getFitToleranceLimits
symbols.signature: (nominalSize is ValueWithUnits, standard is string, toleranceClass is string, isHoleBasis is boolean)
symbols.description: Retrieves fit tolerance limits for a given nominal size, tolerance class, and basis.

parameters.name: nominalSize
parameters.description: The nominal size with units for which the fit tolerance limits are required.

parameters.name: standard
parameters.description: The standard of the fit tolerance table ("ANSI" or "ISO").

parameters.name: toleranceClass
parameters.description: The tolerance class as a string.

parameters.name: isHoleBasis
parameters.description: Indicates whether the basis of the tolerance class is hole or shaft.

returnInfo.description: A map containing the fit tolerance limits. Returns an empty map if no suitable tolerance limits are found.

symbols.name: FixedParameterPosition
symbols.description: Specifies where a constraint should be resolved FIXED_AT_START or FIXED_AT_END will fix the constraint to coincide with the start or end of parametric curves. FIXED_ON_CURVE will fix the help point.

symbols.name: GeometricContinuity
symbols.description: Specifies the level of geometric continuity.

symbols.name: HolePositionReference
symbols.description:
For a `HoleProfile`, a reference point along the hole axis to which the `position` of the profile is relative. To calculate these references, the cylinder of the hole is intersected with the `targets` of the hole. If the hole cylinder encounters a slanted or otherwise irregular face at the intersection with the target, the reference may by a range rather than a single point. Users may specify that they want the end of this range by using a `holeProfile`, or the beginning of this range by using a `holeProfileBeforeReference`; or they may control this behavior directly by setting the `beforeReference` flag of the `HoleProfile`.
Typically, the beginning of the reference range should be used for the first profile of the hole, and the end of the reference range should be used for the rest of the profiles of the hole. Using the beginning of the reference range for the first profile ensures that when the hole tool is cut from a target with a slanted or otherwise irregular entrance face, the first profile is far back enough to ensure that no undesirable overhang is left behind on the target, preventing the fastener from entering the hole. Using the end of the reference range for the rest of the profiles ensures that any nominal distances are measured from where the hole cylinder fully enters the part. As an example of both of these concepts, set the first two profiles of a `HoleDefinition` as a `holeProfileBeforeReference` referencing `TARGET_START` with a `position` of 0 inches, and a `holeProfile` referencing `TARGET_START` with a `position` of 2 inches, respectively. If this layout of profiles is used against a target that has a slanted entrance face, the first profile will be placed right where the cylinder first intersects the part, ensuring that there is no overhang when the hole tool is subtracted from the target. The second profile will be placed 2 inches from where the hole cylinder fully enters the target, such that the request for the hole to be 2 inches deep is understood as the hole having a full two inches of depth in the target, exluding the area where the hole is only partially cut into the target. Notably, to accomplish this the first and second profiles are placed more than two inches apart.
When calculating references, any intersection which is fully behind the `origin` of the axis `Line` (i.e. the axis point) is always ignored. Range intersections where the end of the range is ahead of the axis point but the start of the range is behind the axis point are not ignored.

values.name: AXIS_POINT
values.description: When calling `opHole`, the user will supply one or more `axes` defining where the holes are to be placed. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the `origin` of the axis `Line` of the hole.

values.name: TARGET_START
values.description: When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the closest intersection of the hole cylinder with any target.

values.name: LAST_TARGET_START
values.description: For each target, find the first intersection of the hole cylinder into the target. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest of those intersections. Notably, this means that if certain targets are geometrically constructed such that the hole cylinder enters and exits the target multiple times, only the first of the entrances into the target is considered.

values.name: LAST_TARGET_END
values.description: When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest exit of the hole cylinder from any target.

values.name: LAST_TARGET_START_IN_DEPTH
values.description: For each target, find the first intersection of the hole cylinder into the target. When using this reference, the `position` of the profile will be in reference to the furthest of those intersections within the hole depth.

symbols.name: HoleProfileType
symbols.description: Describes how a `HoleProfile` is constructed in relation to its `HolePositionReference`.

values.name: POSITIONED
values.description: See `holeProfile`.

values.name: MATCHED
values.description: See `matchedHoleProfile`.

symbols.name: Icon
symbols.description: Specifies an icon resource available in Onshape.

values.name: X_LINEAR_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum X linear limit.

values.name: X_LINEAR_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum X linear limit.

values.name: Y_LINEAR_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum Y linear limit.

values.name: Y_LINEAR_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum Y linear limit.

values.name: Z_LINEAR_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum Z linear limit.

values.name: Z_LINEAR_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum Z linear limit.

values.name: X_ROTATION_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum X rotation limit.

values.name: X_ROTATION_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum X rotation limit.

values.name: Y_ROTATION_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum Y rotation limit.

values.name: Y_ROTATION_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum Y rotation limit.

values.name: Z_ROTATION_LIMIT_MIN
values.description: Minimum Z rotation limit.

values.name: Z_ROTATION_LIMIT_MAX
values.description: Maximum Z rotation limit.

values.name: CONE_ANGLE_LIMIT
values.description: Cone angle limit.

values.name: HOLE_DIAMETER
values.description: Hole diameter.

values.name: HOLE_DEPTH
values.description: Hole depth.

values.name: HOLE_DRILL_ANGLE
values.description: Hole drill angle.

values.name: HOLE_COUNTERBORE_DIAMETER
values.description: Hole counterbore diameter.

values.name: HOLE_COUNTERBORE_DEPTH
values.description: Hole counterbore depth.

values.name: HOLE_COUNTERSINK_DIAMETER
values.description: Hole countersink diameter.

values.name: HOLE_TAP_DIAMETER
values.description: Hole tap diameter.

values.name: HOLE_COUNTERSINK_ANGLE
values.description: Hole countersink angle.

values.name: HOLE_TAPPED_DEPTH
values.description: Hole tapped depth.

values.name: HOLE_TAP_CLEARANCE
values.description: Hole tap clearance.

values.name: ALONG_X
values.description: Along the X-axis.

values.name: ALONG_Y
values.description: Along the Y-axis.

values.name: ALONG_Z
values.description: Along the Z-axis.

values.name: DOF_TRANSLATION
values.description: Degrees of freedom translation.

values.name: DOF_ROTATION
values.description: Degrees of freedom rotation.

values.name: DIHEDRAL_LOW
values.description: Dihedral low.

values.name: DIHEDRAL_HIGH
values.description: Dihedral high.

symbols.name: LoftTopology
symbols.description: Specifies topology option for opLoft.

values.name: MINIMAL
values.description: Minimal number of faces created.

values.name: COLUMNS
values.description: One face is created for each matching set of profile segments.

values.name: GRID
values.description: Faces created for COLUMNS option are split at each profile.

symbols.name: LookupTablePath
symbols.description:
A `LookupTablePath` identifies a map of keys into a multi-level table.
The fields on a LookupTablePath depend on its related LookupTable.

symbols.name: canBeLookupTablePath
symbols.description: Typecheck for `LookupTablePath`

symbols.name: lookupTablePath
symbols.description: Creates a `lookupTablePath`.

symbols.name: lookupTableEvaluate
symbols.description: Convert a table expression in string form into a ValueWithUnits.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description:
Currently the following forms are supported: 
 Example: 
`{number} <*> {units}` Example: 
`{number}/{number} <*> {units}` Example: 
`{number} <+> {number}/{number} <*> {units} Where {number} is <-><.>nnn> or <->inf` Example: 
`{} indicate an entity, <> indicate the contents are optional spaces between entities are optional unless they are required to separate entities.`

symbols.name: lookupTableFixExpression
symbols.description: insert plus sign and parenthesis as needed to make a valid expression

symbols.name: getLookupTable
symbols.description: Find a terminal/content table from a path and convert into expression/value form

symbols.name: lookupTableGetValue
symbols.description: Extract the value portion of expression/value maps or evaluate expressions. value maybe an expression or a value with units

symbols.name: isLookupTableViolated
symbols.description: Test if the current definition violates the table.

symbols.name: ManipulatorDragTypeEnum
symbols.description: Specifies the type of manipulator being dragged.

symbols.name: ManipulatorStyleEnum
symbols.description:
Specifies the style of a manipulator intended to look unique. 
 See also: `addManipulators`

values.name: DEFAULT
values.description: the standard display of the manipulator. *

values.name: SECONDARY
values.description: the angular or linear manipulator has two arrow heads. *

values.name: SIMPLE
values.description: the display of the angular or linear manipulator is simpler. *

values.name: TANGENTIAL
values.description: the linear manipulator has two smaller arrows around a circular base.

symbols.name: ManipulatorType
symbols.description: Specifies a specific type of interactive manipulator.

values.name: LINEAR_1D
values.description: A single arrow which can move along a single axis. See `extrude` for an example.

values.name: LINEAR_3D
values.description: A triad of perpendicular arrows which specify a 3D position. See `transformCopy` for an example.

values.name: ANGULAR
values.description: A curved arrow, with two radii, which can move along a circumference to specify an angle. See `revolve` for an example.

values.name: FLIP
values.description: A static arrow which can be clicked to toggle a flip direction. See `extrude` (with BoundingType.THROUGH_ALL) for an example.

values.name: POINTS
values.description: A series of points which can be selected one at a time.

values.name: TOGGLE_POINTS
values.description: A series of points which can each be selected individually.

values.name: TRIAD_FULL
values.description: A triad of perpendicular arrows, planar and angular manipulators.

symbols.name: MateConnectorAxisType
symbols.description: Defines what direction the X-axis of a mate connector should face, relative to the reference defined. Thus, a mate connector defined with `PLUS_Y` will differ from the selected coordinate system by a 90-degree rotation about that coordinate system's Z-axis.

symbols.name: MateDOFType
symbols.description: Specifies a single degree of freedom of a mate.

values.name: Tx
values.description: Translation along the X axis.

values.name: Ty
values.description: Translation along the Y axis.

values.name: Tz
values.description: Translation along the Z axis.

values.name: Rz
values.description: Rotation around the Z axis.

values.name: Ryp
values.description: Rotation around the transformed Y axis from previous transform sequence.

values.name: Rzp
values.description: Rotation around the transformed z axis from previous transform sequence .

symbols.name: MoveCurveBoundaryType
symbols.description: Specifies whether to trim or extend the curve.

symbols.name: OffsetCurveType
symbols.description: Defines what kind of offset distance is used in offset curve on face.

values.name: GEODESIC
values.description: The distance is calculated along the faces of the body in which the selected edges lie in.

values.name: EUCLIDEAN
values.description: The distance is the distance in world coordinates.

symbols.name: OriginCreationType
symbols.description: Specifies how a mate connector origin is defined, and how many entities define it.

symbols.name: PartStudioItemType
symbols.description: Enumeration of the types of items that can be selected inside a Part Studio reference parameter. By default this parameter allows selecting all of the item types below. It can be filtered by specifying a union of any number of PartStudioItemTypes (e.g. `"Filter" : PartStudioItemType.SOLID || PartStudioItemType.ENTIRE_PART_STUDIO`) in the parameter's annotation.

values.name: SOLID
values.description: A body with `BodyType.SOLID` (i.e. a part in the parts list)

values.name: SURFACE
values.description: A non-sketch body with `BodyType.SURFACE` (i.e. a surface in the parts list)

values.name: WIRE
values.description: A non-sketch body with `BodyType.WIRE` (i.e. a curve in the parts list)

values.name: MESH
values.description: An imported mesh (i.e. a mesh in the parts list)

values.name: SKETCH
values.description: An entire sketch feature (i.e. all `POINT`, `WIRE`, and `SURFACE` bodies created by the sketch)

values.name: FLATTENED_SHEET_METAL
values.description: A flattened sheet metal part (available from any Part Studio with sheet metal features)

values.name: ENTIRE_PART_STUDIO
values.description: The entire Part Studio. Setting this option allows clicking the top item (with the full Part Studio's tumbnail and name), which sets the `partQuery` to `qEverything(EntityType.BODY)`.

values.name: CONSTRUCTION_PLANE
values.description: A plane created with Plane feature or with `opPlane`

symbols.name: ProfileControlMode
symbols.description: Specifies how a profile should be controlled while sweeping.

values.name: NONE
values.description: No additional profile control.

values.name: KEEP_ORIENTATION
values.description: Keep the orientation constant.

values.name: LOCK_FACES
values.description: Lock the sweep operation to a set of faces.

values.name: LOCK_DIRECTION
values.description: Lock the sweep operation to a direction.:

symbols.name: ProjectionType
symbols.description: See `opDropCurve`.

symbols.name: PropertyType
symbols.description: Defines the type of property that is being applied to a part.

values.name: CUSTOM
values.description: Requires a custom property id to specify fully.

symbols.name: RecordPatternType
symbols.description: Allows differentiation of patterns in computed data

symbols.name: RotationType
symbols.description: Specifies an axis for rotation.

symbols.name: RuledSurfaceCornerType
symbols.description: Describes how a Ruled Surface creates corners.

values.name: SPUN
values.description: Conical corners are created by spinning an edge.

values.name: EXTENDED
values.description: Ruled surfaces are altered so that they meet at corners.

values.name: LOFTED
values.description: Curvature continuous lofts are created patching corners.

values.name: NO_CORNER
values.description: Leave corners open.

symbols.name: RuledSurfaceType
symbols.description: Describes how a Ruled Surface is defined.

values.name: ANGLE_FROM_FACE
values.description: Ruled surface is perpendicular to reference faces.

values.name: ALIGNED_WITH_VECTOR
values.description: Ruled surface is in direction of reference direction.

values.name: ANGLE_FROM_VECTOR
values.description: Ruled surface will meet a reference direction at an angle when viewed from the path tangent direction.

symbols.name: sectionPart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description:
Feature creating a section of a part behind a plane. Internally, performs an `opSplitPart` followed by an `opDeleteBodies`.
Not exposed in the Part Studio UI.

symbols.name: planeSectionPart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Split a set of parts with a plane and delete all bodies in front of the face. Unlike sectionPart, bodies which are entirely behind the split plane are retained. Any bodies not included in the target query are deleted.

subfields.name: plane
subfields.description: Plane that splits the bodies. Everything on the positive z side of the plane will be removed.

symbols.name: multiplePlaneSectionPart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Split a set of parts with multiple planes and delete all bodies in front of the faces.

subfields.name: sketchPlanes
subfields.description: Planes that splits the bodies. Everything on the positive z side of the planes will be removed.

symbols.name: multiplePlanesSectionTransformedParts
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: method for processing all part studio parts for assembly for multiple planes section

subfields.name: planes
subfields.description: Planes that splits the bodies. Everything on the positive z side of the planes will be removed.

symbols.name: jogSectionPart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Split a part down a jogged section line and delete all back bodies. Used by drawings. Needs to be a feature so that drawings created by queries can resolve. Any bodies not included in the target query are deleted.

subfields.name: sketchPlane
subfields.description: Plane that the jog line will be drawn in and extruded normal to. Everything on the positive x side of the jog line will be removed.

subfields.name: jogPoints
subfields.description: Points that the cutting line goes through in world coordinates.

subfields.name: isPartialSection
subfields.description: Whether or not it is a partial section cut.

subfields.name: keepSketches
subfields.description: Whether or not sketches will be kept in the section cut results.

subfields.name: isBrokenOut
subfields.description: Whether or not it is a broken-out section cut.

subfields.name: isCropView
subfields.description: Whether or not it is a crop section cut.

subfields.name: brokenOutPointNumbers
subfields.description: Array of the number of spline points of each broken-out section cut.

subfields.name: brokenOutEndConditions
subfields.description: Array of end conditions of each broken-out section cut.

subfields.name: offsetPoints
subfields.description: Array of points for offsetting the section lines.

subfields.name: isAlignedSection
subfields.description: Whether or not it is an aligned section cut.

symbols.name: SideGeometryRule
symbols.description: See `opThicken`.

symbols.name: SMCornerType
symbols.description: Specifies the type of corner at a vertex of a sheet metal model.

values.name: OPEN_CORNER
values.description: The corner has more than two bends or, when folded the edges of the metal do not meet.

values.name: CLOSED_CORNER
values.description: The corner has two bends and when folded the edge of the metal meet.

values.name: BEND_END
values.description: The 'corner' is the end of a bend and there may be bend relief.

values.name: NOT_A_CORNER
values.description: The vertex is not associated with a corner

symbols.name: SplitOperationKeepType
symbols.description: See `opSplitPart`.

symbols.name: SurfaceType
symbols.description: The subset of `GeometryType` which applies to 2-dimensional entities

symbols.name: TableTextAlignment
symbols.description: How text in a table column should be aligned

symbols.name: TessellatedLoftReturnStatus
symbols.description: Enumerates failure modes for tessellated loft.

values.name: OK
values.description: No error.

values.name: CRISS_CROSSED_MATCHES
values.description: At least two matches cross each other.

values.name: UNDERNOURISHED_MATCHES
values.description: At least one match consists of fewer than 2 match points.

values.name: NON_CONSECUTIVE_MATCHES
values.description: A match jumps across a profile without specifying a match point.

values.name: MATCHING_CYLINDER_CREATION_FAILED
values.description: Most likely due to two profiles touching.

values.name: NON_CURVE_INPUT
values.description: Only curves are allowed within profiles.

values.name: UNIDENTIFIED
values.description: Some unknown error occurred.

values.name: INVALID_MATCH_POINT_INDEX
values.description: At least one of the match indices points to a non-existent match point.

values.name: INVALID_MATCH_POINT
values.description: At least one match point is specified on non-profile topology.

symbols.name: ToleranceSchemaClass
symbols.description: Used to specify the Onshape standard schema class for a dimension, as in `setDimensionedEntities`.

tool.fs.name: tool
tool.fs.description: The tool.fs module contains enum types shared by multiple features.

symbols.name: MoveFaceType
symbols.description:
Defines how face should be transformed in a `moveFace` feature.
Also used by `boolean`.

symbols.name: ToolBodyType
symbols.description: Defines what type of body a body-creating feature (extrude, revolve, etc.) should create.

symbols.name: ExtendedToolBodyType
symbols.description: Defines what type of body a body-creating feature (extrude, revolve, etc.) should create.

symbols.name: NewBodyOperationType
symbols.description:
Defines how a new body from a body-creating feature (extrude, revolve, etc.) should be merged with other bodies in the context.
To include this enum with the same styling as the extrude dialog (and others), use `booleanStepTypePredicate(definition)`.

values.name: NEW
values.description: Creates a new body in the context with the geometry resulting from the operation.

values.name: ADD
values.description: Performs a boolean union between the new body and all bodies in the merge scope.

values.name: REMOVE
values.description: Performs a boolean subtraction of the new body from all bodies in the merge scope.

values.name: INTERSECT
values.description: Performs a boolean intersection between each new body and each body in the merge scope.

symbols.name: NewSurfaceOperationType
symbols.description:
Defines how a new surface from a surface-creating feature (sweep, loft, revolve, etc.) should be merged with other surfaces in the context.
To include this enum with the same styling as the extrude dialog (and others), use `booleanStepTypePredicate(definition)`.

values.name: NEW
values.description: Creates a new surface in the context with the geometry resulting from the operation.

values.name: ADD
values.description: Performs a surface union between the new surface and all surfaces used as input.

symbols.name: UIHint
symbols.description:
List of available UI Hints, which control how a parameter input is displayed in the feature dialog.
 Example: 

```
annotation { "Name" : "Flip", "UIHint" : UIHint.OPPOSITE_DIRECTION }
definition.isFlipped is boolean;
```

Multiple `UIHint`s can be specified in an array (e.g. `[ UIHint.OPPOSITE_DIRECTION, UIHint.REMEMBER_PREVIOUS_VALUE ]`).
Raw strings (e.g. "OPPOSITE_DIRECTION") may be used in place of the enum access (e.g. UIHint.OPPOSITE_DIRECTION) for the same result.
`OPPOSITE_DIRECTION` and `ALWAYS_HIDDEN` behaviors are considered stable. Other `UIHint`s can be used, but their behaviors may change in future versions of Onshape.

values.name: OPPOSITE_DIRECTION
values.description: For a boolean parameter, display as a toggle button with arrow next to the previous parameter.

values.name: ALWAYS_HIDDEN
values.description: Do not display this parameter or allow a user to edit it (useful for parameters changed only in editing logic or manipulator change functions).

values.name: SHOW_CREATE_SELECTION
values.description: For a query parameter, include a button to open the "Create selection" dialog.

values.name: CONTROL_VISIBILITY
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: NO_PREVIEW_PROVIDED
values.description: For a feature, hide the preview slider which controls before/after transparency, and only show the final version.

values.name: REMEMBER_PREVIOUS_VALUE
values.description: When a user makes a new instance of this feature, set this parameter's default value to the value they set the last time they used this feature.

values.name: DISPLAY_SHORT
values.description: Two consecutive boolean or quantity parameters marked as DISPLAY_SHORT may be placed on the same line.

values.name: ALLOW_FEATURE_SELECTION
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: MATE_CONNECTOR_AXIS_TYPE
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: PRIMARY_AXIS
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: SHOW_EXPRESSION
values.description: If an expression (like #foo or 1/4 in) is entered, always show the expression, and never the resulting value.

values.name: OPPOSITE_DIRECTION_CIRCULAR
values.description: Like `OPPOSITE_DIRECTION`, but with circular arrows.

values.name: SHOW_LABEL
values.description: Show a label above an enum parameter.

values.name: HORIZONTAL_ENUM
values.description: Display an enum as a horizontal list of clickable text, rather than the default select control

values.name: UNCONFIGURABLE
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: MATCH_LAST_ARRAY_ITEM
values.description: Inside an array parameter, set the default value on a new item to match what is set on the last item.

values.name: COLLAPSE_ARRAY_ITEMS
values.description: For an array parameter, create new items (and items in a newly opened dialog) as collapsed by default.

values.name: INITIAL_FOCUS_ON_EDIT
values.description: When an existing feature is edited, the first visible parameter with this UI hint will get focus.

values.name: INITIAL_FOCUS
values.description: When creating or editing, the first visible parameter with this UI hint will get focus (but when editing, a parameter with INITIAL_FOCUS_ON_EDIT takes precedence).

values.name: DISPLAY_CURRENT_VALUE_ONLY
values.description: For Onshape internal use.

values.name: READ_ONLY
values.description: Prevent changes to the parameter from the feature dialog. A read-only parameter can be modified by the editing logic function.

values.name: PREVENT_CREATING_NEW_MATE_CONNECTORS
values.description: For a query parameter allowing BodyType.MATE_CONNECTOR, only allow preexisting mate connectors, and don't provide a button to allow creating new mate connectors specificaly for this parameter.

values.name: FIRST_IN_ROW
values.description: Guarantee that, regardless of other layout requirements, this parameter will always be the first parameter in its displayed row.

values.name: ALLOW_QUERY_ORDER
values.description: Enable reordering of queries in a query parameter.

values.name: PREVENT_ARRAY_REORDER
values.description: Disable reordering of items in an array parameter.

values.name: FOCUS_INNER_QUERY
values.description: When adding a new item to an array parameter, focus the driving inner QLV if the parameter is selection-driven, and focus the first inner QLV otherwise.

values.name: SHOW_TOLERANCE
values.description: For a boolean parameter, display as a toggle button with tolerance icon next to the previous parameter.

values.name: ALLOW_ARRAY_FOCUS
values.description: Allow focusing an array parameter with no driving or inner QLV, as if it were selection-driven.

values.name: SHOW_INLINE_CONFIG_INPUTS
values.description: Inline the configuration parameters in the configure dialog for the feature.

values.name: FOCUS_ON_VISIBLE
values.description: For a query parameter, selects it when it becomes visible during editing.

values.name: CAN_BE_TOLERANT
values.description: For a quantity parameter, specifies that the quantity can be tolerant.

values.name: CAN_BE_TOLERANT_DIAMETER
values.description: For a quantity parameter that represents a diameter, specifies that the quantity can be tolerant and should display options to specify hole or shaft fit classes.

values.name: PLUS_MINUS
values.description: Like `OPPOSITE_DIRECTION` but indicating whether the associated quantity is positive or negative.

values.name: ALWAYS_USE_DEPENDENCIES
values.description: Always use the content of this parameter to determine feature dependencies.

values.name: NO_QUERY_VARIABLE
values.description: Hides the query variable dropdown for the associated query parameter.

values.name: ALLOW_FLAT_SKETCH_SELECTION
values.description: Allows a feature list parameter to select sketches defined on flat views.

symbols.name: VariableType
symbols.description: See `assignVariable`.

symbols.name: VolumeAccuracy
symbols.description: Level of accuracy to use for computing the volume of a solid body. The values are:  LOW: fastest, least accurate, good for approximations MEDIUM: moderately fast, moderately accurate HIGH: slowest, most accurate, required for regeneration

symbols.name: WeldFinishing
symbols.description: Enum to determine the weld finishing in ANSI standard.

symbols.name: WeldStandard
symbols.description: Enum to switch the weld symbol representation between ANSI and ISO standard.

symbols.name: WrapType
symbols.description: Specifies the type of wrap to execute.

values.name: SIMPLE
values.description: Wrap around the infinite definition of the destination surface.

values.name: TRIM
values.description: Wrap around a defined destination face, and trim anything that falls off of it.

values.name: IMPRINT
values.description: Imprint edges onto a defined destination face.
